failure.root_cause: The hash-reviewed final plan bound /home/raymond/q2-rollout/runtime/baseq2/pak0.pak as --stock-pak. That base archive has SHA-256 1ce99eb11e7e251ccdf690858effba79836dbe5e32a4083ad00a13ecda491679, while both committed q2dm1-through-q2dm8 authorities require the retail pak1.pak SHA-256 678210ecd1b27dde1c645660333a1a7b139d849425793859657f804d379b62ad. The final driver pinned the arbitrary input digest but did not compare it with the corpus authorities before consuming source authorization. Source through generated promotion completed 28/28; the atomic stock wrapper then rejected its nested Atlas builder at the PAK/provenance digest check. The wrapper preserved only a generic 66-byte stderr and removed private scratch, exposing a second forensic-diagnostics defect. Source authorization is consumed, so every 71454 declaration, map, seed, source, compiled, materialized, claim, Atlas, report, marker, and passing subset is permanently forensic-only and cannot be retried, resumed, reused, copied forward, salvaged, substituted, or admitted. A successor requires pre-source PAK-to-provenance/inventory digest binding, bounded nested stderr in terminal evidence, a completely fresh disposable qualification, a separately committed disjoint declaration, and a new one-shot lifecycle.
